skillName: Use and interpret inequalities in context (incl number lines and statements)
misconceptionSummary: Students reverse inequality direction when ordering mixed negatives and zero.

steps.narration: The student put zero first, then the negatives. But negative numbers are all less than zero — they all belong to the left of zero on the number line.
Plot all four values on the line. Reading from left to right gives the ascending order automatically. Notice all negatives are to the left of zero.
All negatives come before zero. Among the negatives, the more negative value — further from zero — comes first. Then zero. Then all positives.
Negative seven, negative three, zero, two. The number line reads left to right — and that order is always ascending.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: placing negatives after zero in ascending order. All negatives are less than zero — they all go to the left of it.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: In ascending order, negatives always come first. Zero is in the middle. Positives come last. The number line shows this immediately.
